edges.description: Unresolved meiotic recombination intermediates block germ-cell progression through prophase I and trigger germ-cell apoptosis.
edges.source: Impaired crossover formation at meiotic recombination intermediates
edges.target: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: In 46,XX individuals, germ-cell depletion exhausts the ovarian follicle pool and produces primary ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion
edges.target: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals, the same germ-cell depletion produces spermatogenic failure presenting as non-obstructive azoospermia.
edges.source: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: The female branch presents clinically as primary or premature ovarian insufficiency, with variable severity from poor ovarian response to complete ovarian failure.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals, severe spermatogenic arrest manifests clinically as non-obstructive azoospermia.

edges.target: Impaired crossover formation at meiotic recombination intermediates

nodes.description: Biallelic HFM1 loss disables the helicase activity required to process recombination intermediates into designated crossovers during meiotic prophase I. HFM1 stabilizes the MutSγ complex (MSH4-MSH5) on Holliday junctions and promotes class I crossover formation. Its loss leads to varying reductions in HFM1 foci on chromosome axes and defects in synapsis and crossover formation, blocking meiotic progression.
nodes.id: Impaired crossover formation at meiotic recombination intermediates

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic HFM1 variants cause non-obstructive azoospermia with meiotic arrest in humans by impairing crossover formation to varying degrees.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic variants in HFM1 cause human male infertility owing to non-obstructive azoospermia (NOA) with impaired crossover formation and meiotic metaphase I (MMI) arrest.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic HFM1 variants cause non-obstructive azoospermia with meiotic arrest in humans by impairing crossover formation to varying degrees.
evidence.snippet: These Hfm1 variants led to various reductions of HFM1 foci on chromosome axes and resulted in varying degrees of synapsis and crossover formation defects in the mutant male mice.

nodes.description: Germ cells that cannot complete meiotic crossover formation arrest at meiotic metaphase I and are eliminated by apoptosis rather than maturing into functional gametes, depleting the germ-cell pool in both sexes.
nodes.id: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic HFM1 variants cause non-obstructive azoospermia with meiotic arrest in humans by impairing crossover formation to varying degrees.
evidence.snippet: Hfm1 mutant female mice displayed infertility or subfertility with oogenesis variously affected.

nodes.description: Loss of oocytes during meiotic crossover failure depletes the ovarian reserve. 46,XX individuals present with primary ovarian insufficiency, poor ovarian response in IVF cycles, and/or recurrent implantation failure. The severity varies: some individuals have amenorrhea and ovarian dysgenesis while others have oligomenorrhea or subfertility.
nodes.id: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Whole-exome sequencing in patients with premature ovarian insufficiency: early detection and early intervention.
evidence.snippet: We recruited 24 patients with POI and identified variants in POI-related genes in 14 patients, including bi-allelic mutations in DNAH6, HFM1, EIF2B2, BNC, and LRPPRC.

evidence.reference_title: Novel deleterious splicing variant in HFM1 causes gametogenesis defect and recurrent implantation failure: concerning the risk of chromosomal abnormalities in embryos.
evidence.snippet: Apart from NOA and POI, biallelic variants in HFM1 were also associated with recurrent implantation failure (RIF).

nodes.description: Reported 46,XX individuals present with primary ovarian insufficiency, often with poor ovarian response, recurrent implantation failure, or complete amenorrhea.

evidence.reference_title: Whole-exome sequencing in patients with premature ovarian insufficiency: early detection and early intervention.
evidence.snippet: We recruited 24 patients with POI and identified variants in POI-related genes in 14 patients, including bi-allelic mutations in DNAH6, HFM1, EIF2B2, BNC, and LRPPRC.

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals, HFM1-dependent meiotic failure produces spermatogenic arrest at meiotic metaphase I. Testicular histology shows complete arrest of spermatogenesis; no retrievable spermatozoa are found on microTESE, and patients present with non-obstructive azoospermia (NOA) or, in some cases with hypomorphic variants, severe oligozoospermia amenable to ICSI.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic HFM1 variants cause non-obstructive azoospermia with meiotic arrest in humans by impairing crossover formation to varying degrees.
evidence.snippet: Testicular histological analysis revealed that spermatogenesis is arrested at MMI in patients carrying the variants.

evidence.reference_title: A novel splicing mutation in helicase for meiosis 1 leads to non-obstructive azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: Our results showed that a deleterious splicing variant in HFM1 was related to NOA in these two patients.
